frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Bro honestly I respect personal choice, but after marriage at least mangalsutra or sindoor should be there no? It just looks proper and shows she values the relation also, aajkal some girls act too modern and then get irritated if elders say anything.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes social pressure on married women to display visible marital markers while framing it as respect, tradition, and maturity rather than control. It encodes the 'personal choice but...', 'looks proper/complete after marriage', and judgment of women who do not wear these markers.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
